title: Beyond Single-shot Writing: Deep Research Agents are Unreliable at Multi-turn Report Revision
authors: Bingsen Chen, Boyan Li, Ping Nie, Yuyu Zhang, Xi Ye, Chen Zhao
problem: Deep research agents are benchmarked single-shot; can they reliably revise long-form reports across multi-turn USER feedback
unit_of_analysis: Deep research agent report across revision turns (5 agents)
intervention: Multi-turn revision driven by human-verified simulated user feedback
method: Unified eval protocol (comprehensiveness/factuality/presentation) + human-verified feedback generation pipeline
data: Long-form research reports across 3 feedback settings; no public controlled evidence worlds
main_claim: Agents address most feedback but regress on 16-27% of previously covered content and citation quality and fail to preserve prior edits
relation_to_ours: Our verified anchor and closest phenomenon-overlap: same 'change-target vs preserve-non-target' tension, BUT input is USER WRITING FEEDBACK not an EVIDENCE-WORLD delta; no gold affected/unaffected claim set tied to an evidence change, no typed dependency graph, no claim-level patch, no metric jointly penalizing stale+spurious revision

title: EditPropBench: Measuring Factual Edit Propagation in Scientific Manuscripts
problem: A local factual edit in a manuscript creates non-local obligations to revise dependent qualitative claims (e.g. 215->80 docs makes 'medium-scale' stale)
unit_of_analysis: Dependent claim pairs within synthetic ML/NLP manuscripts
intervention: LLM editors must propagate a targeted factual edit through dependent claims
method: Controlled fact graph with sentence-level labels (direct targets / required downstream updates / protected unrelated text) + 3 editing protocols + adversarial metric probes
data: Synthetic ML/NLP-style manuscripts with controlled fact graphs
main_claim: Edit-Ripple Adherence (ERA) 0.148-0.705 on implicit stratum; even strongest editors miss ~30% of required cascade updates
relation_to_ours: HIGHEST collision: nearly the same gold-label structure (must-change downstream + must-preserve unrelated) and a metric on both, AND same headline finding. Distinctions that survive: input is an INTERNAL manuscript edit not an EXTERNAL evidence-world delta; no retraction/source-conflict/new-authoritative-source/temporal-validity update types; no conflict/uncertainty (C) representation; no citation or calculation re-validation; synthetic only (no real versioned evidence); it is a benchmark, not a typed-edge dependency-graph METHOD; no deep-research agent in the loop and no audit trail

title: FRUIT: Faithfully Reflecting Updated Information in Text
authors: Robert L. Logan IV, Alexandre Passos, Sameer Singh, Ming-Wei Chang
problem: Keeping textual knowledge bases (Wikipedia) up to date by suggesting edits grounded in new external evidence
unit_of_analysis: Wikipedia article being updated given new evidence
intervention: Generation task: update an existing article so it reflects new evidence
method: EDIT5 (T5-based editing model) trained on distantly supervised snapshot pairs; UpdateROUGE metric scoring only updated sentences
data: FRUIT-WIKI 170K distantly-supervised pairs + 914-instance gold eval set
main_claim: EDIT5 sets SOTA on faithfully integrating new evidence into existing articles
relation_to_ours: Original 'update an existing document given new evidence' task, directly in our family. Distinctions: no claim-level dependency graph / multi-hop derived or computed claims; no gold PRESERVE set or metric penalizing spurious revision (UpdateROUGE rewards only changed sentences); no retraction/conflict/temporal-validity taxonomy; pre-deep-research-agent, single-model generation not agentic patch+audit

title: DeepFact: Co-Evolving Benchmarks and Agents for Deep Research Factuality
authors: Yukun Huang, Leonardo F. R. Ribeiro, Momchil Hardalov, Bhuwan Dhingra, Markus Dreyer, Venkatesh Saligrama
problem: No benchmark evaluates claim-level factuality verifiers on deep research reports; gold labels themselves are unreliable
intervention: Audit-then-Score (AtS): verifiers submit evidence to dispute labels; auditors adjudicate; accepted revisions update a versioned benchmark
main_claim: Versioned DRR factuality benchmark; expert micro-gold accuracy rises 60.8%->90.9% over 4 audit rounds
relation_to_ours: Shares the deep-research-report object and a VERSIONED/auditable benchmark idea, BUT it VERIFIES/SCORES claim factuality (and revises BENCHMARK LABELS), it does not REVISE a report when the underlying evidence is updated; no evidence-delta->affected-claim mapping, no patch generation, no preserve-vs-change metric on report content

title: Evaluating the Ripple Effects of Knowledge Editing in Language Models
authors: Roi Cohen, Eden Biran, Ori Yoran, Amir Globerson, Mor Geva
problem: Model-editing evals only check the edited fact and unrelated facts; they ignore logically-derived facts that must also change
unit_of_analysis: Single factual edits to a model's parametric knowledge
intervention: Edit a fact in model parameters and test downstream logically-implied facts
method: RippleEdits diagnostic benchmark (5K edits across 6 ripple types) + in-context editing baseline
main_claim: Editing methods fail to update logically-derived facts; simple in-context editing beats specialized editors
relation_to_ours: Conceptual ancestor of our 'derived/multi-hop claims must change' requirement and locality (preserve unrelated), BUT it is MODEL (parameter) editing evaluated via QA, not revision of a long-form report/document; no evidence world, citations, retraction, conflict, temporal validity, or patch+audit; no preservation penalty on prose

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Conflicting Evidence
authors: Han Wang, Archiki Prasad, Elias Stengel-Eskin, Mohit Bansal
problem: RAG must jointly handle ambiguity misinformation and noise / conflicting documents
unit_of_analysis: User query with a retrieved set of conflicting/noisy documents
intervention: MADAM-RAG multi-agent debate + aggregation to disambiguate and suppress misinformation
method: RAMDocs dataset simulating ambiguity+misinformation+noise; eval on AmbigDocs/FaithEval
main_claim: Overlaps our 'conflicting evidence -> honest C set' sub-problem, BUT it is single-turn QA over a static conflicting set, not REVISION of an existing report; no temporal evidence delta (W_t -> W_t+1), no affected/unaffected report-claim labels, no preservation metric

title: When Facts Change: Probing LLMs on Evolving Knowledge with evolveQA
authors: Nishanth Sridhar Nakshatri, Shamik Roy, Manoj Ghuhan Arivazhagan, Hanhan Zhou, Vinayshekhar Bannihatti Kumar, Rashmi Gangadharaiah
problem: Static benchmarks unfairly compare LLMs and ignore temporal knowledge conflicts when facts evolve
unit_of_analysis: QA pairs tied to LLM knowledge cutoff dates
intervention: Build temporally-grounded QA from timestamped real corpora (AWS/Azure/WHO)
method: evolveQA benchmark from naturally-occurring knowledge evolution
main_claim: LLMs drop up to 31% on evolving-knowledge questions vs static
relation_to_ours: Shares real timestamped evidence evolution (close to our Layer B real versioned worlds), BUT unit is QA not a report; it MEASURES parametric staleness, it does not REVISE a report given an evidence delta; no dependency graph, no preserve/change patch, no citation/calculation validity

title: Generation-Time vs. Post-hoc Citation: A Holistic Evaluation of LLM Attribution
authors: Yash Saxena, Raviteja Bommireddy, Ankur Padia, Manas Gaur
problem: Which citation strategy yields trustworthy attributed LLM outputs
unit_of_analysis: Citations attached to LLM outputs under G-Cite vs P-Cite paradigms
intervention: Compare generation-time vs post-hoc citation across datasets
method: Holistic eval (coverage vs correctness vs latency) over 4 attribution datasets
main_claim: P-Cite gives high coverage with competitive correctness; recommend retrieval-centric P-Cite
relation_to_ours: Touches citation faithfulness / post-rationalization (a dimension we maintain via citation re-validation), BUT it is about attaching citations during generation, not maintaining citation validity through an evidence update; no report revision, no evidence delta, no claim dependency graph

title: From Fluent to Verifiable: Claim-Level Auditability for Deep Research Agents
authors: Razeen A Rasheed, Somnath Banerjee, Animesh Mukherjee, Rima Hazra
problem: For deep research agents the bottleneck is tracing which sentence is supported by which passage (auditability)
unit_of_analysis: Individual claims and their evidence links in agent reports
intervention: Auditable Autonomous Research (AAR) standard with persistent queryable provenance graphs
method: Measurement framework: provenance coverage/soundness + contradiction transparency + audit effort
data: Not a fixed dataset; a standard + provenance graph
main_claim: Auditability is the new bottleneck; claim-evidence linking must be a first-class design goal
relation_to_ours: Closest on the 'claim-evidence (provenance) graph + audit trail' machinery for deep research, BUT it targets GENERATION-TIME auditability/provenance, not selective REVISION under an evidence delta; no W_t->W_t+1 update, no gold affected/unaffected sets, no patch generation, no change-vs-preserve metric

title: ChainEdit: Propagating Ripple Effects in LLM Knowledge Editing through Logical Rule-Guided Chains
authors: Zilu Dong, Xiangqing Shen, Zinong Yang, Rui Xia
problem: Knowledge editing methods lose logical consistency when propagating edits to dependent facts
unit_of_analysis: Model parametric knowledge and dependent fact clusters
intervention: Use knowledge-graph-derived logical rules to chain-edit logically connected facts
method: Logical-rule extraction aligned with LLM reasoning to edit knowledge clusters
main_claim: 30%+ improvement in logical generalization while preserving reliability/specificity
relation_to_ours: Method analogue of 'use a graph of typed relations to propagate updates to dependent claims', BUT it edits MODEL PARAMETERS not a report; no document, evidence world, citations, retraction, conflict, temporal validity, or audit; QA evaluation only

title: CiteCheck: Retrieval-Grounded Detection of LLM Citation Hallucinations in Scientific Text
authors: Khashayar Khajavi, Shaghayegh Sadeghi, Rise Adhikari, Alexander Tessier
problem: LLM scientific text contains plausible but corrupted or fabricated citations
intervention: Retrieve candidate publications then structured LLM comparison + calibrated labels
method: Hybrid retrieval + structured comparison mapping to Exact/Minor/Major
relation_to_ours: Represents the 'citation correctness detection' area our manual explicitly excludes from novelty; DETECTION only, no revision, no evidence delta, no dependency graph, no preserve/change patch
